{{Short description|Film award ceremony in 2004}} {{Use mdy dates|date=February 2025}} {{Infobox film awards | number = 61 | award = Golden Globe Awards | image = | caption = | date = January 25, 2004 |site=[[Beverly Hilton Hotel]]
[[Beverly Hills]], [[Los Angeles]], [[California]]| best_film_drama = ''[[The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King]]'' | best_film_musical_or_comedy = ''[[Lost in Translation (film)|Lost in Translation]]'' | best_drama_series = ''[[24 (TV series)|24]]'' | best_musical_or_comedy_series = ''[[The Office (UK TV series)|The Office]]'' (UK) | best_miniseries_or_tv_movie = ''[[Angels in America (miniseries)|Angels in America]]'' | last = 60th | next = 62nd | network = [[NBC]] }} The '''61st [[Golden Globe Award]]s''', honoring the best in [[film]] and television for 2003, were held on January 25, 2004, at the [[Beverly Hilton Hotel]] in [[Beverly Hills]], [[California]].{{cite news|last1=Hayes|first1=Dade|title=By Gollum, it’s ‘The King’|url=https://variety.com/2004/film/awards/by-gollum-it-s-the-king-1117899023/|access-date=10 January 2018|work=Variety|date=25 January 2004}}{{cite news|title='Return of the King' dominates Golden Globes|url=http://edition.cnn.com/2004/SHOWBIZ/Movies/01/25/golden.globes/|access-date=10 January 2018|work=CNN|date=26 January 2004}}{{cite news|last1=Welkos|first1=Robert|last2=King|first2=Susan|title=Gold rush is on|url=https://www.latimes.com/archives/la-xpm-2004-jan-26-et-welkos26-story.html|access-date=10 January 2018|work=Los Angeles Times|date=26 January 2004}} The nominations were announced on December 18, 2003.{{cite news|title=Globes high on 'Cold Mountain'|url=http://edition.cnn.com/2003/SHOWBIZ/Movies/12/18/globe.noms/index.html|access-date=10 January 2018|work=CNN|date=18 December 2003}}{{cite news|last1=Welkos|first1=Robert|last2=King|first2=Susan|title='Cold' call from Globes|url=https://www.latimes.com/archives/la-xpm-2003-dec-19-et-globes19-story.html|access-date=10 January 2018|work=Los Angeles Times|date=19 December 2003}}{{cite news|title=And the nominees are...|url=https://www.latimes.com/archives/la-xpm-2003-dec-19-et-globelist19-story.html|access-date=10 January 2018|work=Los Angeles Times|date=19 December 2003}} ''[[The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King]]'' won the most awards, with 4 (including Best Motion Picture – Drama). ''[[Cold Mountain (film)|Cold Mountain]]'' received the most nominations, with 8 (winning 1, Best Supporting Actress). ''[[Big Fish]]'' had the most nominations (4) without a single win. The ceremony was watched by a record-setting audience of 26.8 million people.{{cite web|url=http://www.jamshowbiz.com/TelevisionRatings/us.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20040203065228/http://www.jamshowbiz.com/TelevisionRatings/us.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=2004-02-03|title=Golden Globes set NBC ratings record|date=January 28, 2004|publisher=[[Associated Press]]}} ==Winners and nominees== [[File:SeanPennAAFeb09.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Sean Penn]], Best Actor in a Motion Picture – Drama winner]] [[File:Charlize Theron @ 2010 Academy Awards crop2.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Charlize Theron]], Best Actress in a Motion Picture – Drama winner]] [[File:Bill Murray, Monuments Men premiere.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Bill Murray]], Best Actor in a Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy winner]] [[File:Diane Keaton 2012-1 (cropped).jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Diane Keaton]], Best Actress in a Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy winner]] [[File:TimRobbinsTIFFSept2012.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Tim Robbins]], Best Supporting Actor winner]] [[File:Renée Zellweger Berlinale 2010 (cropped).jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Renée Zellweger]], Best Supporting Actress winner]] [[File:Anthony LaPaglia crop.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Anthony LaPaglia]], Best Actor in a Television Series – Drama winner]] [[File:Frances Conroy at PaleyFest 2014 - 13491478183.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Frances Conroy]], Best Actress in a Television Series – Drama winner]] [[File:Ricky Gervais 2010.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Ricky Gervais]], Best Actor in a Television Series – Musical or Comedy winner]] [[File:Sarah Jessica Parker at Miami Rhapsody 30th Anniversary Celebration.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Sarah Jessica Parker]], Best Actress in a Television Series – Musical or Comedy winner]] [[File:Al Pacino.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Al Pacino]], Best Actor in a Miniseries or Television Film winner]] [[File:Meryl Streep by Jack Mitchell.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Meryl Streep]], Best Actress in a Miniseries or Television Film winner]] [[File:Jeffrey Wright by Gage Skidmore.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Jeffrey Wright]], Best Supporting Actor in a Series, Miniseries, or Television Film winner]] [[File:Mary-Louise Parker by Gage Skidmore.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Mary-Louise Parker]], Best Supporting Actress in a Series, Miniseries, or Television Film winner]] These are the nominees for the 61st Golden Globe Awards.
